scope.purpose: Set review gates for Beta historical, cultural, language, naming, title, symbol, and uncertainty records before formal content fill.
scope.betaContentUse: No formal Beta real-world claim is accepted by this baseline. Later fill tasks must attach source records and labels before use.

priorityOrder: edited contemporary or near-contemporary primary sources
university press or equivalent academic monographs
museum, archaeology, or university collection records
specialist reference works for synthesis only
sourcePolicy.fullTextRule: If only metadata, table of contents, or abstract is available, record accessStatus as ABSTRACT_OR_METADATA_ONLY and do not mark the claim HISTORICAL_ACCEPTED.
sourcePolicy.pageRule: Formal content claims need page, chapter, inscription, catalogue number, section, or stable collection record. If not yet verified, set pageOrSection to RESEARCH REQUIRED.

sourceRecords.notes: Project synthesis for shared context. It is not a page-verified formal source database.
Defines source classes, claim records, uncertainty labels, violence-cost requirements, and scout limitations.
Bibliography metadata and research leads. Page-level verification remains required before formal content fill.

sourceRecords.citation: Victor B. Lieberman, Burmese Administrative Cycles: Anarchy and Conquest, c. 1580-1760, Princeton University Press, 1984
sourceRecords.notes: Candidate for administrative cycles and post-1580 political analysis; not page-verified in this baseline.

sourceRecords.citation: Victor Lieberman, Ethnic Hatred and Universal Benevolence: Ethnicity and Loyalty in Precolonial Myanmar, and Britain, Comparative Studies in Society and History 63(2), 2021
sourceRecords.notes: Candidate source for avoiding modern ethnic-loyalty shortcuts; not page-verified in this baseline.

sourceRecords.citation: Michael Aung-Thwin, A Tale of Two Kingdoms: Ava and Pegu in the Fifteenth Century, Journal of Southeast Asian Studies 42(1), 2011
sourceRecords.notes: Candidate source for checking older ethnicized Ava/Pegu narratives; not page-verified in this baseline.

sourceRecords.citation: Deborah E. Tooker, Putting the Mandala in its Place: A Practice-Based Approach, Journal of Asian Studies 55(2), 1996
sourceRecords.notes: Candidate source for critique of universal mandala abstractions; not page-verified in this baseline.

sourceRecords.citation: Chris Baker and Pasuk Phongpaichit, A History of Ayutthaya: Siam in the Early Modern World, Cambridge University Press, 2017
sourceRecords.notes: Candidate source for Ayutthaya society, commerce, and politics; not page-verified in this baseline.

sourceRecords.citation: Jacques P. Leider, Le Royaume d'Arakan (Birmanie): son histoire politique entre le debut du XVe et la fin du XVIIe siecle, EFEO, 2004
sourceRecords.notes: Candidate source for Arakan/Mrauk U political history; not page-verified in this baseline.

sourceRecords.citation: Michael W. Charney, Southeast Asian Warfare, 1300-1900, Brill, 2004
sourceRecords.notes: Candidate source for military organization, firearms, and campaign practice; not page-verified in this baseline.

claimRecords.claim: M7 historical review covers the working 1531-1600 western and central mainland Southeast Asian frame, but this frame is not a formal border, polity roster, or content lock.
claimRecords.label: INFERRED
sourceStatements: The project baseline defines the working time and space frame and warns against treating modern borders as historical facts.
scholarlyInterpretations: Later formal content must verify each polity, place, date, and title from source-specific evidence rather than inheriting the project overview.
claimRecords.researcherInference: Use the working frame only as a review envelope for later claim records.
competingInterpretations: A narrower 1531 opening or a broader regional map may be justified later, but either requires task approval and source records.
claimRecords.gameAbstraction: Permit scenario and map candidates to reference the frame as COMPOSITE or RESEARCH REQUIRED until page-level evidence exists.

claimRecords.claim: The content pipeline must not describe the 16th-century conflict space as fixed modern nations or ethnic blocs.
claimRecords.label: INFERRED
sourceStatements: Project policy rejects modern border back-projection and static ethnic job or battle templates.
scholarlyInterpretations: Candidate scholarship listed by the project bibliography is flagged for later verification against older nationalist, victor-chronicle, and colonial historiography frames.
claimRecords.researcherInference: Until verified, UI and content should prefer polity, court, office, locality, kinship, religious, language, and patronage links over modern-national labels.
competingInterpretations: Some sources may use broad ethnonyms as period terms.
Modern nationalist or colonial historiography may turn those terms into fixed political identities.
claimRecords.gameAbstraction: Culture, language, religion, and polity labels may inform reviewed context only; they must not create fixed buffs, penalties, or faction templates.

claimRecords.claim: Mandalic or galactic polity language is an analytical aid, not a universal production rule for every region and date.
claimRecords.label: INFERRED
sourceStatements: The project baseline warns not to turn mandala into a universal formula.
scholarlyInterpretations: Candidate scholarship in the bibliography critiques over-spatialized mandala models and requires verification before formal use.
claimRecords.researcherInference: Model concrete suzerain links, obligations, distance, routes, offices, and local agency instead of a single center-periphery template.
competingInterpretations: A court-centered model may explain some ceremonial or tributary relationships.
Practice-based local evidence may show more negotiated or fragmented authority.
claimRecords.gameAbstraction: Use explicit vassal, obligation, route, and legitimacy records; do not use a hidden mandala aura mechanic.

claimRecords.claim: The existing 1531, 1569, and 1581 scenario anchors are M6 Alpha scaffolding and must not be treated as formal M7 content lock records.
claimRecords.label: COMPOSITE
sourceStatements: Project baseline lists suggested scenario years and marks the timeline as a working skeleton requiring later verification.
scholarlyInterpretations: Formal scenario content needs triangulated page-level claim records for persons, dates, titles, places, and causal claims.
claimRecords.researcherInference: Keep all current Alpha scenario IDs and display keys as validation scaffolding until M7 fill replaces or accepts them through review.
competingInterpretations: M7 may retain the anchors with verified sources.
M7 may narrow, fictionalize, or replace them if evidence or cultural review requires it.
claimRecords.gameAbstraction: The anchors may validate systems and workflows, not final historical truth.

claimRecords.claim: Forced migration, slavery, massacre, famine, religious conflict, and wartime population loss must record victims and long-term consequences when represented.
claimRecords.label: INFERRED
sourceStatements: Project policy requires victim, source-region, household, production, integration, resistance, and long-term political-cost handling.
scholarlyInterpretations: Population movement may be historically significant to state resources, but the game must not convert it into a painless reward.
claimRecords.researcherInference: Any later mechanic or event involving coercive population movement needs explicit cost ledgers and cultural review.
competingInterpretations: Some primary chronicles may present coercion from a victor perspective.
Later scholarship may emphasize source-community loss, resistance, and state fragility.
claimRecords.gameAbstraction: Short-term labor, cash, military, or control gains must be paired with population, legitimacy, relationship, resistance, and memory consequences.

claimRecords.claim: Names, titles, language strings, transliterations, aliases, and display names require a style guide and source record before formal content use.
claimRecords.label: RESEARCH REQUIRED
sourceStatements: Project baseline requires stable IDs, original text or scholarly transliteration, player display names, aliases, and consistent style guidance.
scholarlyInterpretations: Language and title choices need specialist review because common English spellings may mix traditions and periods.
claimRecords.researcherInference: Do not invent local-style spellings, honorifics, office names, or title hierarchies in M7 fill.
competingInterpretations: Research records may preserve multiple original or scholarly transliteration forms.
claimRecords.gameAbstraction: Use stable ASCII IDs and localization keys; keep display names review-blocked until language review passes.

claimRecords.claim: Clothing, architecture, weapons, music, religious imagery, court ritual, flags, and symbols cannot be produced from generic regional style assumptions.
claimRecords.label: RESEARCH REQUIRED
sourceStatements: Project baseline prioritizes contemporary imagery, archaeology, museum objects, and specialist research over modern reconstructions.
scholarlyInterpretations: Material culture must be dated and regionalized; modern national tradition cannot be directly projected into the 16th century.
claimRecords.researcherInference: Audio/art/localization completion must carry asset-reference manifests and culture-specific review before acceptance.
competingInterpretations: A historically inspired original asset can be acceptable if labeled as such.
A reconstruction claim needs period-specific evidence.
claimRecords.gameAbstraction: Permit placeholder-neutral icons only when explicitly marked FICTIONAL or COMPOSITE and not sacred or culture-specific.

claimRecords.claim: Religion and ritual should not be reduced to simple hostility, static buffs, or UI decoration.
claimRecords.label: INFERRED
sourceStatements: Project baseline notes Theravada Buddhist institutions, Brahmanical court elements, local spirit or ancestor traditions, and Muslim commercial or court connections may coexist.
scholarlyInterpretations: Specific ritual, monastic, title, and iconographic claims require specialized research and cultural review.
claimRecords.researcherInference: Religious content should enter as reviewed institutions, legitimacy relationships, patronage, education, mediation, or audience interpretation, not a conflict toggle.
competingInterpretations: Court rituals may be described through dynastic legitimacy sources.
Local religious practice may require different evidence and cultural readers.
claimRecords.gameAbstraction: Use explicit reviewed consequences and no consumable sacred-symbol UI unless approved.

reviewGates.minimumEvidence: Every real-world claim must point to a sourceRecord and pageOrSection, or be explicitly tagged RESEARCH REQUIRED.
reviewGates.humanGateWhen: A major unresolved cultural-risk decision would otherwise be accepted.

reviewGates.minimumEvidence: At least two interpretations or an explicit RESEARCH REQUIRED block for contested or high-risk material.
reviewGates.humanGateWhen: The project must choose among major live interpretations or override a culture-risk request.

reviewGates.minimumEvidence: Stable ID, localization key, source form or scholarly transliteration, display form, language/context note, character limit, and review state.
reviewGates.humanGateWhen: A public-facing name or title carries major cultural or political risk.

reviewGates.minimumEvidence: Victim group, source region, immediate loss, integration or resistance, long-term legitimacy/relationship/economic effects, and content warning route when needed.
reviewGates.humanGateWhen: The content would make coercion a cost-free optimal reward or minimize victim consequences.

reviewGates.minimumEvidence: Mechanics must cite explicit reviewed relationships, institutions, offices, logistics, or costs rather than fixed group traits.
reviewGates.humanGateWhen: A proposed exception would encode a high-risk identity shortcut.

languageBaseline.stableIdRule: Use lowercase ASCII dotted IDs. Never use display names as references.
languageBaseline.displayNameRule: Display names, title strings, aliases, and glosses must be review records, not hard-coded facts.

mustScanFor: victor chronicle causality treated as neutral fact
colonial-era ethnic categories treated as timeless
unsupported certainty in dates, titles, clothing, and institutions
humanGateRequiredFor: turning disputed high-risk material into accepted certainty
using sacred or religious imagery as UI decoration or consumable resource
approving a coercive policy as a dominant reward path without victim and long-term consequences

downstreamTasks.M7-SCENARIO-PERSON-EVENT-FILL-001: Must fill formal scenario, person, and event records only with labels, claimIds, sourceIds, localization keys, and review states.
downstreamTasks.M7-AUDIO-ART-LOCALIZATION-COMPLETE-001: Must produce asset/reference manifests and route culture-specific symbols, sounds, clothing, architecture, and names through review.
downstreamTasks.M7-BETA-CULTURE-REVIEW-001: Must independently review high-risk records and route unresolved major cultural decisions to Human Gate.
downstreamTasks.M7-CONTENT-LOCK-ACCEPTANCE-001: Must synthesize evidence and request explicit Human Gate approval; this baseline cannot approve lock.
